mdx.body:
import CodeWrapper from "../../../src/components/Mdx/CodeWrapper" # Overview of the Pseudo Classes #### A good place to start Ok, so you've decided on a design, you've installed the classes, you have your colors picked out, you have your fonts picked out, and need to get started. I figured a good place to start off these actual docs is with the Pseudo Classes! The framework comes with 3 default element staes: - Hover - Active - and Focus If that's not enough, take a look at the [Customize](/customize/) section to add your own! It's as easy as adding the correct element-state name to this list in `_variables.scss`: ```scss $pseudo-classes: "hover", "active", "focus"; ``` So, for example if you want a `target` pseudo class, it's as easy as adding it to the list. However, given how particular the use-cases of many of these are, I think it would make more sense for most cases to take the opportuinty to write your own SCSS for these cases. Let's take a look at some examples. Maybe it would be fun to make a button:
Here is the HTML: ```html
``` I would like to draw your attention to the `hover:bg-green-lt` class. This basically says to apply the bg-green-lt class on hover. Pretty simple huh? Let's try another button this time with a box shadow:
```html
``` This time the backgroud reacts to the `active` pseudo class. Notice that the shadow also disappears, since it too reacts to the active class. Also, if your eyes are sharp, you might notice that `transition-fast-colors` from the first example has become `transition-fast`. The first applies transitions only to things with colors, but the second applies `transition: all`. Even though there IS a transiion class for box shadows (`transition-[speed]-shadow`), when transitioning multiple unrelated properties it's best to either use the `transition-[speed]` class to `transition: all` (either that or write your old css class). For a final exmaple, let's try an input to see the Focus class in action:
```html
``` I applied focus properties to the background and the text color sith the `focus:bg-blue` and `focus:text-white` classes. Hopefullly this has provided an instructive overview! Take a look at the [Colors](/docs/colors/), [Classes with Colors](/docs/classes-with-colors/), and [Additional Classes](/docs/additional-classes/) pages for more in-depth examples.
